display.details: FamilySearch and Cantwell forum snippets place Sarah Alice Cantwell with Barnabas/Barney Cantwell, but the exact maternal side is not yet cleanly named from open snippets. Treat this as a target for Coshocton County, Ohio and Iowa records.

display.details: FamilySearch indexes Barnabas/Barney Cantwell as son of Thomas Cantwell Jr. and Jemima Kelley; a Cantwell forum lead pushes Thomas to William Cantwell and Margaret O'Brien of Dublin, but that is beyond Gen 6 and unproven here.

display.details: FamilySearch, WikiTree, and MotherBedford identify Samuel Claar Sr. as son of George Washington Claar and Barbara House in the Klar/Claar line. This remains compiled-lineage evidence until original Bedford/Jackson County records are checked.

FamilySearch and Treespot place Samuel Dove Claar as son of Henry Claar and Electa Alice White. Henry was born 31 Dec 1838 in Jackson County, Ohio and died 28 Dec 1915 in Topeka, Kansas.{{cite:src.samuel-dove-claar-familysearch.55d8177a}}{{cite:src.henry-claar-familysearch.073900d4}}{{cite:src.henry-claar-and-electa-a-white-family-group-treespot.84bb1661}}

display.details: Treespot and MotherBedford identify Henry Claar as son of Samuel Claar and Lydia Stropes. MotherBedford gives Samuel born 12 Mar 1800 and married Lydia Stropes on 29 Oct 1827.{{cite:src.motherbedford-klar-claar-genealogy-page.ebad933d}}{{cite:src.henry-claar-and-electa-a-white-family-group-treespot.84bb1661}}

display.details: FamilySearch page for Samuel Dove Claar names spouse Mary Margaret Hoyle, gives their marriage as 5 Oct 1895 in Oberlin, Decatur County, Kansas, and lists Martha Frances Claar among children.{{cite:src.samuel-dove-claar-familysearch.55d8177a}} A Decatur County marriage index gives the same groom, date, and place but lists the bride as Maggie M. Hogle.{{cite:src.decatur-county-kansas-groom-marriage-index-c-f-ksgenweb.e5ee851c}} The index fixes the place and date, but its groom and bride views repeat one volunteer transcription rather than independent evidence. Hoyl, Hoyle, Hogle, and Hayle all remain variants until the original marriage record is inspected.
display.identity: Donna's Claar grandparents; Hoyle-vs-Hogle conflict active.
display.title_override: Samuel Dove Claar + Mary Margaret Hoyle {{vs}} Maggie M. Hogle {{case:case.17}}
display.vitals: m. 5 Oct 1895, Oberlin, Decatur County, Kansas

display.details: Named as Evelyn's mother in Evelyn's obituary.{{cite:src.evelyn-delores-zimmerman-obituary-baalmann-mortuary.e1d95407}} The Colorado county marriage record (Washington Co, report no. 1896) records her marrying Homer C. Mundell at Akron, Colorado, on 19 Jun 1933 as “Marjorie M. Clemans,” aged 21; the Colorado Divorce Index closes the marriage on 26 Jun 1939 and gives her fuller name as Marjorie Evelyn Mundell — the source of her daughter Evelyn's name. Her own parents remain unproven. Four same-name “Marjorie Clemans” women have now been examined and excluded: the Barron County, Wisconsin daughter of Talmadge Clemans (b. ~1922, too young); Marjorie Opal Clemans of Liberty Mills, Indiana (b. 1914, m. Clay V. Stamper 1935){{cite:src.marjorie-opal-clemans-familysearch.f092de64}}{{cite:src.descendants-of-christian-eby-internet-archive-text.0f8669ad}}; Marjorie E. Clemans of Kit Carson County, Colorado (b. 1918, daughter of Laura Clemans); and Marjorie Emily Clemans of Waynoka, Oklahoma (b. 1915, daughter of Benjamin F. Clemans, who married a Weaver, never a Mundell). See {{link:#case.07|case.07}}.
display.identity: Evelyn's mother in the Clemans branch.
display.title_override: Marjorie Evelyn Clemans Mundell

display.details: Donna's obituary names Chester and Martha Claar Connelly as parents.{{cite:src.donna-lea-connelly-dible-obituary-baalmann-mortuary.a2afb9a6}} Chester is indexed as 20 Oct 1891-22 Nov 1981, Rexford, Kansas.
display.identity: Donna's parents; Martha's Claar row is {{link:#person.claar.martha|C-3}}.
display.title_override: Chester Franklin Connelly + Martha Frances Claar
display.vitals: Chester · b. 20 Oct 1891 – d. 22 Nov 1981, Rexford, Kansas

display.details: Find a Grave listings for James Connelly and Laura Inis Connelly Walls, plus the FamilySearch page for Nola Temperance Connelly, place Chester Franklin Connelly in the James Connelly and America Ellen Durham household.{{cite:src.james-connelly-find-a-grave.1b3c0aa2}}{{cite:src.nola-temperance-connelly-familysearch.3db19104}} WKU's Muhlenberg County Heritage article lists America Ellen Durham as born 25 Aug 1858, died 28 Jan 1947, married James Connelly.{{cite:src.muhlenberg-county-heritage-vol-5-no-1.3ab8ae71}}
display.vitals: America · b. 25 Aug 1858 – d. 28 Jan 1947

display.details: FamilySearch sibling pages place Ray Hershel Dible among children of Harry H. Dible and Julia Dible. Julia's profile says she was daughter of John Heckman Dible and Elizabeth J. Haden and married Harry on 10 Jun 1896 in Trenton, Hitchcock County, Nebraska.{{cite:src.julia-dible-familysearch.4e444220}}
display.vitals: m. 10 Jun 1896, Trenton, Hitchcock County, Nebraska

display.details: A three-page compiled Dible family paper places John Dible, 1805-1866, husband of Catherine Heckman, as son of Jacob Dible, 1780-1872, and Susannah Allshouse. It identifies Susannah as daughter of Henry Allshouse and a Truxall mother. The same paper points beyond this displayed Gen 6 to Johanne Jacob Dreibelbis, a 1732 immigrant, but that step still needs independent proof.
display.title_override: Likely Jacob Dible + Susannah Allshouse

display.details: FamilySearch pages for Zephaniah's siblings list John Albert Dibler and Catherine Heckman as parents.{{cite:src.john-heckman-dible-find-a-grave.959d1e08}}

display.title_override: John Albert Dibler/Dible + Catherine Heckman

display.details: FamilySearch snippet for Julia Dible names John Heckman Dible and Elizabeth J. Haden as parents.{{cite:src.julia-dible-familysearch.4e444220}}{{cite:src.john-heckman-dible-familysearch.45ce2091}}
Bill's obituary names Ray and Almeda Long Dible as his parents.{{cite:src.william-j-bill-dible-obituary-baalmann-mortuary.46fec901}}

display.details: The 1860 census enumerates Catherine Dible, age 21 and Pennsylvania-born, with Zephaniah in Allegheny Township, Armstrong County, supporting birth about 1839.{{cite:src.1860-united-states-census-zephaniah-and-catherine-dible-household.a6d85618}} In 1886, daughter Mary E. Dible signed a marriage application naming Zephaniah and Catherine Dible as her parents; a 1901 biography of son John independently calls his mother Catherine Moore and says Zephaniah later remarried.{{cite:src.1886-marriage-application-of-mary-e-dible.a8b4f7ba}}{{cite:src.history-of-wapello-county-iowa-and-representative-citizens.c9420f9b}} No reliable record yet proves Catherine's death year, burial, or parents; the published 1864 and 1872 dates remain unsupported conflicts.
display.identity: Bill's Dible great-grandparents; Catherine Moore dates conflict.
display.vitals: Catherine · b. ~1839, Pennsylvania – alive 12 Jul 1860 – death year and burial open {{case:case.10}}

cameos.alt: Doyle and Evelyn Zimmerman on their wedding day, June 1954
Grave marker of Doyle J. Zimmerman, Sgt US Army, Korea

display.details: 1930-2005, Selden/Colby, Kansas. Find a Grave profile names Leonard Henry Zimmerman and Cecilia Antoinette Zodrow Zimmerman as parents.{{cite:src.doyle-jule-zimmerman-find-a-grave.a6b1ff2c}} Family testimony gives the middle name as Julius; the corrected Zodrow parentage makes Julius Henry Zodrow his maternal great-uncle, not his grandfather. The Find a Grave memorial renders the name "Jule" and his grave marker abbreviates to "Doyle J." The 14 Jun 1953 marriage date traces to Evelyn's 2023 obituary, written after both spouses had died; the other public sources showing 1953, including both Find a Grave memorials and the Legacy.com mirror, all copy that obituary text, so none is an independent witness. The couple's own 50th-anniversary announcement in the Colby Free Press of 11 Jun 2004, published while both could correct it, establishes 14 Jun 1954 at Sacred Heart Catholic Church, and its timeline, with the couple meeting in January 1954, corroborates the correction.{{cite:src.doyle-and-evelyn-zimmerman-50th-anniversary-announcement-colby-free-pr.ffe48c56}} His military grave marker records service as a sergeant in the US Army in Korea. His memorial places the burial at Beulah Cemetery, Colby, and lists brothers Leonard Francis Zimmerman, 1928-2010, and Cloyd Leo "Zim" Zimmerman, 1936-2009.
display.vitals: b. 1930 – m. 14 Jun 1954, Sacred Heart Catholic Church – d. 2005

display.details: WKU's Muhlenberg County Heritage family record gives David Monroe Durham, born 28 Dec 1832 in North Carolina, died 24 Aug 1897 in Nebo, Kentucky, married Virginia Ann Ford on 19 Aug 1857 in Menard County, Illinois, and lists America Ellen Durham as their child.{{cite:src.muhlenberg-county-heritage-vol-5-no-1.3ab8ae71}} A separate 1911 Ford family letter in Muhlenberg County Heritage says Virginia Ann Ford was born 27 Dec 1842 and married David M. Durham in February 1858, so the marriage date/place still needs the original Menard County record.{{cite:src.muhlenberg-county-heritage-vol-3-no-1.9f02f87f}} Public child pages also point to this couple, but date/name variants appear: a FamilySearch page for son David Milton Durham calls the father David Monroe Durham Jr., and a Find a Grave index snippet for Mary Isabella Durham Hibbs gives father David Monroe Durham with 1829-1887 dates. Keep the America-to-David/Virginia link, but resolve David's exact identity with original marriage, death, probate, and cemetery records before moving upward.{{cite:src.mary-isabella-durham-hibbs-find-a-grave.d10f195a}}
display.vitals: David · b. 28 Dec 1832, North Carolina {{vs}} 1829 – d. 24 Aug 1897, Nebo, Kentucky {{vs}} 1887 {{case:case.15}} – Virginia · b. 27 Dec 1842

display.details: 1935-2023, born Flagler, Colorado, died Colby, Kansas. Evelyn's obituary names Homer and Marjorie (Clemans) Mundell as parents and lists brothers Cy, Don, Jerry, Rich, and Walt, all of whom predeceased her.{{cite:src.evelyn-delores-zimmerman-obituary-baalmann-mortuary.e1d95407}} The 1940 U.S. Census (Barrett Township, Thomas County, Kansas) enumerates Evelyn, age 5, and a younger brother, Herbert Duane Mundell (1936–1972), as grandchildren in the household of Lon and Frances Adolph — the primary record behind the obituary's account that Evelyn was raised by her grandmother. Herbert's death is confirmed by the Social Security Death Index.
display.title_override: Evelyn Delores Mundell Zimmerman
display.vitals: b. 1935, Flagler, Colorado – d. 2023, Colby, Kansas

display.details: A 1911 Muhlenberg Sentinel letter reprinted in WKU's Muhlenberg County Heritage was written by their son Rev. G. W. Ford. It says John Laborn Ford was born 11 Oct 1811 in Caswell County, North Carolina, rode to Greenville, Kentucky in 1839, met Lucy Ann Staples at Friendship church, married her in April 1840, and had children including Virginia Ann Ford.{{cite:src.muhlenberg-county-heritage-vol-3-no-1.9f02f87f}} Other compiled/index leads point to a 30 Mar 1842 Kentucky marriage.{{cite:src.caswell-county-historical-association-john-laborn-ford-note.537a9805}} Treat the couple and child link as strong, but pull the original Muhlenberg/Menard marriage and probate records before treating either marriage date as final.
display.title_override: Likely John Laborn/Laban Ford + Lucy Ann Staples
display.vitals: John · b. 11 Oct 1811, Caswell County, North Carolina – m. Apr 1840 {{vs}} 30 Mar 1842, Kentucky {{case:case.16}} – res. 1839, Greenville, Kentucky

display.details: Elizabeth J. Haden Dible's Find a Grave page names parents Andrew Jackson Haden and Sophia Reid. KaysAncestry agrees, gives Sophia as Sophia Reed, places the family in Cumberland County, Kentucky census households, and shows Elizabeth/Betty in an 1880 Nebraska John Dible household with Kentucky-born Richard Hayden likely her brother. The Andrew page adds a Cumberland County deed clue: Andrew J. Haden mortgaged stock and crops to Sally L. Haden, identified there as his mother. Treat the family as a working lead until original census, marriage, deed, probate, or death records are pulled.
display.title_override: Likely Andrew Jackson Haden + Sophia Reid/Reed

display.details: KaysAncestry and FamilySearch identify Andrew Jackson Haden as son of Richard D. Haden and Sarah "Sally" Lewis Tompkins. KaysAncestry gives Richard as born 30 Jun 1776 in Albemarle County, Virginia, married Sally on 7 Mar 1799 in Albemarle County, and died 15 Feb 1837 in Cumberland County, Kentucky; Sally died 15 Oct 1841 in Cumberland County. This is a good Gen 6 Haden layer, but it should be hardened with the Albemarle marriage bond and Cumberland County deed/probate records.
display.title_override: Likely Richard D. Haden + Sarah "Sally" Lewis Tompkins
display.vitals: Richard · b. 30 Jun 1776, Albemarle County, Virginia – m. 7 Mar 1799, Albemarle County – d. 15 Feb 1837, Cumberland County, Kentucky – Sally · d. 15 Oct 1841, Cumberland County

display.details: FamilySearch indexes Catherine Heckman as daughter of Philip Heckman and Esther/Maria Esther Otterman; sibling pages for George and John Jacob Heckman repeat the same parent pair. A sibling memorial for Ludwig "Louis" Heckman says Philip was son of Albrecht Heckman, immigrant from Boedigheim, Mosbach, Baden, Germany, arriving at Philadelphia aboard the Beulah on 10 Sep 1753 per the compiled memorial bio, but that is beyond this displayed Gen 6 and needs original church/probate proof.
1901-1962. Marriage notice links Almeda Long and Ray H. Dible in 1922 Jackson County, Kansas.{{cite:src.jackson-county-kansas-marriage-index.9c3a5c57}}
display.title_override: Almeda Ora Long Dible
display.vitals: b. 1901 – m. 1922, Jackson County, Kansas – d. 1962

display.details: Chapman's 1880 History of Pike County (validated 2026-07-08) states John F. Long was born in Tennessee in 1805, son of Robert and Betsy (Wasson) Long, both natives of North Carolina, and married Sally A. Patton of Vanderburgh County, Indiana (born there 1809) on 23 Feb 1826; among their eight children Chapman lists “Robert N.” — identified with Robert Nelson Long (m. Ann Ghent Sleight, 1857) on father, county, and chronology.{{cite:src.chapman-pike-county-1880.6975c649}} Corrected on a July 2026 re-read of Rev. Robert Long's Find a Grave biography: the memorial names only two sons, John Franklin Long, 1805-1882, born Tennessee, died Montezuma Township, Pike County, Illinois, and Abner Nelson Long, and lists Robert Nelson Long as John Franklin's second child, making Rev. Robert his grandfather, not his father as earlier noted here.{{cite:src.rev-robert-long-find-a-grave.3aea8e6c}} Rev. Robert, 1775-1850, and Betsy Wasson appear as natives of North Carolina in the 1880 Chapman county history, and Robert died at Abingdon, Locust Grove Township, Jefferson County, Iowa, buried Smith Branch Cemetery. Still needs a probate, Bible, or land record before proof status.
display.title_override: Likely John Franklin Long + Sally A. Patton
display.vitals: John · b. 1805, Tennessee – d. 1882, Montezuma Township, Pike County, Illinois – Rev. Robert · b. 1775 – d. 1850, Abingdon, Locust Grove Township, Jefferson County, Iowa

display.details: Find a Grave biography says Robert Nelson Long married Ann Ghent Sleight on 19 Feb 1857 in Griggsville Township, Pike County, Illinois. The Rev. Robert Long memorial bio gives Robert Nelson's span as 16 Nov 1835, Greenpond, Pike County, Illinois to 27 Apr 1911, Jackson County, Kansas, and places him in the 1910 census in Jackson County in the household of daughter-in-law Cora E. McClellan Long, independently corroborating the Long-McClelland chain.{{cite:src.robert-nelson-long-find-a-grave.1e583c65}}{{cite:src.rev-robert-long-find-a-grave.3aea8e6c}}
display.vitals: Robert · b. 16 Nov 1835, Greenpond, Pike County, Illinois – m. 19 Feb 1857, Griggsville Township, Pike County, Illinois – d. 27 Apr 1911, Jackson County, Kansas – res. 1910, Jackson County

display.details: FamilySearch sibling page for Nelson Leonard Long identifies William Wasson Long and Cora Ellie McClelland as parents of Almeda's sibling group. Find a Grave has William Walker Long, 1862-1908, spouse Cora Ellie McClellan; his memorial gives death 15 Sep 1908 in Los Angeles, California, and hers gives birth 8 Dec 1872 at Fairfield, Jefferson County, Iowa and death 19 Mar 1952 at Manhattan, Riley County, Kansas.{{cite:src.william-walker-long-find-a-grave.2dc04924}}{{cite:src.cora-ellie-mcclellan-long-find-a-grave.4819b565}}
display.title_override: William Walker/Wasson Long + Cora Ellie McClelland
display.vitals: William · b. 1862 – d. 15 Sep 1908, Los Angeles, California – Cora · b. 8 Dec 1872, Fairfield, Jefferson County, Iowa – d. 19 Mar 1952, Manhattan, Riley County, Kansas

display.details: WikiTree, Find a Grave, and FamilySearch leads identify Lovina Parker Love as daughter of Thomas Louis Love and Hannah Ann James. FamilySearch's Thomas Lewis Love profile gives Thomas 1796-1871, Hannah Ann James 1805-1871, a 5 Feb 1828 marriage, and a child list including Lovina Parker Love. This needs Athens County, Ohio marriage, probate, land, or church support before it becomes more than a compiled Gen 5 lead.
display.title_override: Likely Thomas Louis Love + Hannah Ann James

display.details: WikiTree leads identify Dorsey Overturf McClelland as son of Alexander M. McClelland and Sarah Hoge. The working trail puts Alexander about 1801 at Fayette City/Fayette County, Pennsylvania, a marriage to Sarah Hoge about 1822 in Greene County, Pennsylvania, and death about 7 Feb 1863 at Bishopville/Athens County, Ohio; Sarah is shown as born in 1803, died in 1892, and buried at Mount Olive Cemetery near Troy, Doniphan County, Kansas. This needs Pennsylvania/Ohio/Kansas primary confirmation.
display.title_override: Likely Alexander M. McClelland + Sarah Hoge
display.vitals: Alexander · b. ~1801, Fayette City/Fayette County, Pennsylvania – m. ~1822, Greene County, Pennsylvania – d. ~7 Feb 1863, Bishopville/Athens County, Ohio – Sarah · b. 1803 – d. 1892

display.details: Find a Grave listing for Cora Ellie McClellan Long gives these parents, and the Selden Cemetery transcription places Dorsey O. McClelland on a matching concrete-base marker with Ora E. McClelland and Almeda O. Dible. Working geography is now much sharper: Dorsey appears in compiled leads as born 26 Sep 1825 in Greene County, Pennsylvania and died 19 May 1904 at Selden, Kansas; Lovina appears as born 28 Oct 1832 in Trimble/Athens County, Ohio, married Dorsey on 18 Nov 1850 at Hartleyville, Athens County, Ohio, and died 23 Dec 1883 in Osborne County, Kansas. Original marriage, census, probate, and cemetery images are still needed.{{cite:src.selden-cemetery-ksgenweb.82a81b07}}{{cite:src.cora-ellie-mcclellan-long-find-a-grave.4819b565}}{{cite:src.dorsey-overturf-mcclelland-find-a-grave.99e95448}}
display.vitals: Dorsey · b. 26 Sep 1825, Greene County, Pennsylvania – m. 18 Nov 1850, Hartleyville, Athens County, Ohio – d. 19 May 1904, Selden, Kansas – Lovina · b. 28 Oct 1832, Trimble/Athens County, Ohio – d. 23 Dec 1883, Osborne County, Kansas

display.details: FamilySearch, OCCGS, and the St. Charles County Historical Society compiled page identify John Pryor Mundell as a child of Harvey William Mundell and Elizabeth Jane Brown. Harvey was born in April 1837 in Clinton County, Indiana, married Elizabeth Jane Brown on 27 Dec 1857 in Clarke County, Iowa, served in Company G, Iowa 34th Infantry, and later lived in Iowa, Kansas, Oklahoma, and California.
display.vitals: b. Apr 1837, Clinton County, Indiana – m. 27 Dec 1857, Clarke County, Iowa

display.details: Named as Evelyn's father.{{cite:src.evelyn-delores-zimmerman-obituary-baalmann-mortuary.e1d95407}} Born about 1911 in Kansas; the 1930 census finds him a nineteen-year-old farm hand lodging in Cloverly, Big Horn County, Wyoming. He married Marjorie Evelyn Clemans at Akron, Colorado, on 19 Jun 1933 (Washington Co marriage record no. 1896), and the Colorado Divorce Index records their divorce on 26 Jun 1939. By the 1940 census he was a mechanic in Colby, Thomas County, Kansas, remarried to a much younger second wife, Leora; he married a third time, to Clara Isbell Walker, at Colorado Springs on 6 Feb 1956. The 1950 Knob Hill census places Homer and Leora with sons Jerry, Walter, and Donald plus a daughter left unnamed here; the named sons were Evelyn's half-brothers, while Evelyn and her full brother Herbert remained with the Adolph grandparents. William Rogers in Donald's obituary remains unexplained outside the Colorado marriage index.{{cite:src.donald-lynn-mundell-obituary-fry-funeral-home.820e326b}} (The 1933 record recorded his age as 26, four years high; his 1930 and 1940 census ages agree on ~1911.)
display.title_override: Homer Clair/Claire Mundell

display.details: Compiled household sources place Harvey William Mundell as a son of John Mundell and Deborah Elmore, but that edge still lacks an original record. One generation higher is now firmer: the official Clinton County Courthouse Archives index identifies John as a son in James Mundell's 1837 probate folder, and a 1968 transcript of Will Book I, page 22, independently matches.{{cite:src.1837-james-mundell-probate-index-clinton-county-courthouse-archives.89a85610}}{{cite:src.descendants-of-james-mundell-and-his-wife-margaret-garrett-mundell.5f04b6fe}} {{case:case.22}} keeps Harvey-to-John open and targets the original probate file.

Public FamilySearch and Find a Grave entries place Walter William Mundell in this household; the OCCGS Harvey Mundell Civil War profile and St. Charles County page also track John Pryor as a child of Harvey William Mundell and Elizabeth Jane Brown.{{cite:src.walter-william-mundell-familysearch.bd666c67}}{{cite:src.john-pryor-mundell-find-a-grave.e1163e68}}{{cite:src.occgs-harvey-mundell-civil-war-veterans-project-pdf.af694281}}

The 1940 U.S. Census (Barrett Township, Thomas County, Kansas) enumerates Evelyn and her brother Herbert as grandchildren of Lon and Frances Adolph — and those grandchildren can reach Frances only through her son Homer, which proves that “Frances Adolph” is Mary Frances “Frances” Rust, Homer's mother, remarried to Lon Adolph. This is the record the earlier working answer wanted: it confirms the Rust→Adolph step Evelyn's obituary implies, and the Rust-family forum's account that Frances later married Lyle Strawn and lived at Beloit, Kansas. Walter William Mundell is named as her first husband and Homer's father by FamilySearch and Mundell/Cantwell forum leads, with the 1910 marriage placed at Smith Center; the maternal identity is now well documented, but that paternal step still wants an original marriage or census record. (The 1940 census gives Frances's age as 45 and birthplace Missouri, against the FamilySearch 1894 and the Smith County Rust cluster — a field to reconcile.){{cite:src.evelyn-delores-zimmerman-obituary-baalmann-mortuary.e1d95407}}{{cite:src.walter-william-mundell-familysearch.bd666c67}}{{cite:src.mary-frances-rust-forum-post-genealogy-com-archive.0356175c}}
display.vitals: Frances · b. 1894 – m. 1910, Smith Center, Kansas – d. 1984

display.details: Born 7 Apr 1872 in Waterloo, Ontario per FamilySearch; died 25 Sep 1935 (stone: 9-25-1935); buried at Selden Cemetery as Elizabeth A. Zimmerman, same stone as Michael J. Zimmerman. The shared Selden Cemetery stone reads 4-7-1871 while the FamilySearch index gives 7 Apr 1872; the year discrepancy is case.04.{{cite:src.elizabeth-catherine-nauer-familysearch.66c2d521}}{{cite:src.selden-cemetery-ksgenweb.82a81b07}}
display.title_override: Elizabeth Catherine Nauer Zimmerman
display.vitals: b. 7 Apr 1872, Waterloo, Ontario {{vs}} 4-7-1871 {{case:case.04}} – d. 25 Sep 1935

display.details: The original 1851 Canada census now places Lorenz Nauer, 49, and Thorothea Nauer, 50, both Prussia-born Roman Catholics, in one Wilmot Township household with Lorenz and Maria Wand and four younger Nauer children. Thomas is absent, and the census does not state relationships. This validates the blended Nauer/Wand household but does not prove Thomas as a son or stepson or settle the Meyer-versus-Wand naming conflict.{{cite:src.1851-canada-census-lorenz-nauer-household-wilmot-township.bc7cc74a}} The next useful proof is Thomas's 1856 marriage or a related Catholic baptism or burial that names his parents.
display.title_override: Possible: Johann Lorenz Nauer + Dorothea Meyer {{vs}} Mary Dorothea Wand {{case:case.04}}
display.vitals: Lorenz · b. ~1803 – Dorothea · b. ~1802 – res. 1851/52, Wilmot Township – res. 1861, Carrick Township, Bruce County

cameos.alt: Photo from the Find a Grave memorial of Moritz Thomas Nauer, son of Thomas and Catherina
cameos.credit_label: Photo on son Moritz's memorial: Find a Grave

display.details: FamilySearch indexes Elizabeth's father as Thomas A. Nauer and her mother as Catherine Marie; sibling and cemetery-index evidence gives the fuller name Catherina Marie Koeberger/Koburger. Moritz Thomas Nauer's memorial gives Thomas A. Nauer, 1824-1884, and Catherina Koeberger Nauer, 1832-1906; Catherina's own memorial gives birth 4 Mar 1832 and death 1 Feb 1906. The Waterloo German Catholic Settlers PDF places Thomas and Catharine in Waterloo County in 1871 and Cawker, Mitchell County, Kansas by 1880. Son Moritz, whose memorial photo appears on this row, was born 14 Sep 1873 at Maryhill, Ontario, married Louisa Catherine Zimmerman in 1897, possibly the Louisa of John Paul Zimmerman's 1880 Norfolk household, and died 14 May 1953 at Jennings, Decatur County, Kansas.{{cite:src.moritz-thomas-nauer-find-a-grave.048597c8}}{{cite:src.the-german-catholic-settlers-of-waterloo-county-pdf.73dd582b}}
display.vitals: Thomas · b. 1824 – d. 1884 – Catherina · b. 4 Mar 1832 – d. 1 Feb 1906 – res. 1871, Waterloo County – res. 1880, Cawker, Mitchell County, Kansas

display.details: The upstream link from John F. Rust remains a working hypothesis. Frauke Folkerts Everwyn, not Nomdje Meiner, was John Aissen Rust's wife and F. John's proposed mother; Nomdje was John's second wife and remains a collateral spouse only. The 1830 burial of Jan Aissen Rust and 1841 marriage of Aisse Jansen Rust concern two older people and no longer stand in for John Aissen born in 1842. {{case:case.06}} targets a direct record tying F. John to John Aissen and Frauke.
display.title_override: Possible John Aissen Rust + Frauke Folkerts Everwyn

display.details: Resolved 2026-07-09. The 1900 and 1905 censuses enumerate the same three children — Francis, Clarence, and William — with John F. and Ethel/Etta Rust. After John's 1909 death, the 1910 census directly calls Francis Etta's daughter and places Walter Mundell as a boarder in the household during the year he and Frances married. That three-record sequence establishes John F. Rust and Ethel B. Rupert as Mary Frances's parents.{{cite:src.william-bryan-rust-find-a-grave.95e736d4}}{{cite:src.reamsville-cemetery-interment-net.656f3e01}} {{case:case.06}} now tracks only the upstream John Aissen Rust and Frauke Folkerts Everwyn hypothesis.
display.vitals: John · b. 12 Apr 1870 – d. 18 Oct 1909

display.details: Find a Grave biography for Robert Nelson Long identifies Ann Ghent Sleight's parents as John Gaunt Sleight and Rebecca Rachel Walker, both associated with Lincolnshire, England.{{cite:src.robert-nelson-long-find-a-grave.1e583c65}}

Rev. G. W. Ford's 1911 letter names Lucy Ann Staples as daughter of John Burton Staples and Nancy Ann Turner. It gives John Burton as born 25 Jun 1785 in Virginia, Nancy Ann as born in November 1782 in Virginia, their marriage in May 1818, a move to East Tennessee about 1830, settlement about three miles southeast of Greenville in fall 1835, and deaths in June 1867 and February 1882. It also places both in Friendship Missionary Baptist Church. This is stronger than a generic tree lead, but probate, land, church minutes, and the marriage record still need pulling.{{cite:src.muhlenberg-county-heritage-vol-3-no-1.9f02f87f}}
display.title_override: Likely John Burton Staples + Nancy Ann Turner

display.details: Corrected 2026-07-08: {{url:https://www.findagrave.com/memorial/104955721|Cecilia's memorial}} gives her Leoville birth, Colby death, Sacred Heart burial, and parents Leonard Ferdinand Zodrow and Catherine Fleckenstein Zodrow.{{cite:src.cecilia-zodrow-zimmerman-find-a-grave.5586b816}} {{url:https://www.findagrave.com/memorial/71839302|Leonard Ferdinand Zodrow's memorial}} reciprocally lists Cecilia among his children and places Leonard in the John Zodrow + Antonette Grams family.{{cite:src.leonard-ferdinand-zodrow-find-a-grave.7870a1b4}} Primary-tier negatives corroborate the cutover: no Washington certificate in Julius Henry Zodrow's household names Cecilia among the documented children, and Hattie Zodrow Schroer's 1961 obituary places brother Leonard at Selden, Kansas. The older public FamilySearch sibling-indexing basis that made Julius Henry Zodrow + Ernestina Baier her parents is superseded. The 1910 US Census (Sheridan Co, ED 0187, sheet 6A) enumerates her as "Celia A", age 5, a daughter in Leonard's household with siblings Leo and Mary — primary confirmation (case.21 CLOSED).
display.identity: Doyle's mother in the Zodrow branch.
display.title_override: Cecilia Antoinette Zodrow Zimmerman

display.details: Leonard Ferdinand Zodrow's memorial places him in the John Zodrow and Antonette Grams family; Julius Henry Zodrow's memorial and sibling memorials corroborate the same sibling group. FamilySearch indexes John as born 29 Jun 1843 in Prussia/Germany, and the Leoville Calvary Cemetery transcription gives the more specific birthplace "Sabanka Prussia." Sabanka itself is not resolving in Meyers, GOV, or Nominatim. The best usable county/parish/town target remains Neu Lebehnke / Nowa Lubianka, village in Kreis Deutsch Krone, Regierungsbezirk Marienwerder, Westpreussen, with civil registry at Lebehnke and church targets at Lebehnke / Stara Lubianka. Meyers identifies Neu Lebehnke as a Dorf and points to Standesamt Lebehnke; Meyers identifies Lebehnke as having Catholic and Protestant parish churches; AGOFF's 1885 Kreis Deutsch Krone table lists Neu Lebehnke with evangelical church, Catholic church, and Standesamt all at Lebehnke. The Deutsch Krone Project shows a local Zodrow cluster; Lebehnke Catholic banns name Zodrow collateral families there; and a Polish archive catalog points to a Johann Zodrow property file in Neu Lebehnke, 1860-1873. This is still a locality hypothesis, not proof of John's birth parish yet. Antonette's memorial gives her birth as 23 May 1846 in Germany, a marriage to John in 1867, death 1 Aug 1934 at Dresden, Decatur County, Kansas, and names her mother as Rosa (Rolof) Grams, 1802-1895 - the first parent lead on the Grams side.{{cite:src.leonard-ferdinand-zodrow-find-a-grave.7870a1b4}}{{cite:src.john-zodrow-familysearch.35a4f123}}{{cite:src.antonette-grams-zodrow-find-a-grave.46e00498}}{{cite:src.leoville-calvary-cemetery-transcription-ksgenweb.6d88b722}}
display.identity: Doyle's Zodrow great-grandparents; Sabanka/Lebehnke is unresolved.
display.title_override: John Zodrow + Antonia/Antonette Grams
display.vitals: John · b. 29 Jun 1843, Prussia/Germany {{vs}} Sabanka Prussia {{case:case.01}} – m. 1867 – Antonia · b. 23 May 1846, Germany – Antonia · d. 1 Aug 1934, Dresden, Decatur County, Kansas

display.details: Doyle's great-uncle's household - previously miscarried as Cecilia's parents; corrected 2026-07-08. Julius born June 1869 in Wisconsin per his memorial (his 1959 death certificate, State File 6169, gives 9 Jun 1870 — a one-year conflict), months after the family's reported 1868 arrival, and died 13 Mar 1959 at Medical Lake, Spokane County, Washington.{{cite:src.julius-henry-zodrow-find-a-grave.b2c95ae2}}{{cite:src.julius-zodrow-wa-death-certificate.d9291309}} The Washington certificate cluster keeps this as Julius and Ernestina's collateral household: Albert L., Leonard William, and August Henry are documented there, with Gordon appearing as August Henry's informant; no Cecilia appears in those certificates. Ernestina died in mid-May 1940 at Chewelah, Stevens County, Washington, per the state death index — which also names her father, August Biaer [i.e. Baier] — correcting the compiled “1963” previously shown here; the Holy Cross register's 11 May burial date conflicts impossibly with the index's 14 May death date, so the exact day is left open (2026-07-08 validation, Washington Digital Archives).{{cite:src.ernestine-zodrow-wa-death-index.24690954}}
display.identity: Doyle's great-uncle's household; not an ancestor row.
display.title_override: Julius Henry Zodrow + Ernestina Baier
display.vitals: Julius · b. Jun 1869 {{vs}} 9 Jun 1870 {{case:case.01}}, Wisconsin – d. 13 Mar 1959, Medical Lake, Spokane County, Washington – Ernestina · b. 1872 – d. 14 May 1940 {{vs}} bur. 11 May 1940 {{case:case.01}}, Chewelah, Stevens County, Washington

display.details: {{url:https://www.findagrave.com/memorial/71839302|Leonard Ferdinand Zodrow's memorial}} gives his Shawano birth, Colby death, Selden Cemetery burial, parents John Zodrow + Antonette Grams, spouse Catherine Fleckenstein, and children including Cecilia Antoinette Zodrow Zimmerman.{{cite:src.leonard-ferdinand-zodrow-find-a-grave.7870a1b4}} {{url:https://www.findagrave.com/memorial/104955721|Cecilia's memorial}} reciprocally names Leonard and Catherine as her parents.{{cite:src.cecilia-zodrow-zimmerman-find-a-grave.5586b816}} The Selden burial matters because it keeps the corrected Zodrow household in the same Sheridan/Thomas County corridor as Doyle's 1930 Selden birth.
display.vitals: Leonard · b. 1 Nov 1874, Shawano, Shawano County, Wisconsin – d. 10 Oct 1961, Colby, Kansas – Catherine · b. 1874 – d. 1961
